title: Interstellar Relay: Beam-Alignment Saddle Optimizer
isolation: candidate runs as an isolated stdin->stdout subprocess via isorun; the hidden saddle matrices, ground-truth equilibria, and evaluator state stay in the parent process
strategies: reproduce the reference extragradient step 1/(2L) -> ~0.1 on every relay class (no design)
constant near-edge extragradient step 0.9/L -> beats the conservative reference on well-conditioned relays but wastes budget on rotation-dominated / ill-conditioned ones

structure-adaptive schedule: near-edge extragradient step + heavy-ball momentum tuned to mu/L on symmetric ill-conditioned classes, momentum suppressed on rotation-dominated (bilinear/mixed) classes where it diverges -> strong across the whole family
per-step decaying / restarted schedules, optimistic-gradient (OGDA) coefficients, or Nesterov-style acceleration expressed through the (eta_t, alpha_t, beta_t) arrays
